Write a forum convo noting that one show that only airs in mornings will be taking the 8:30pm slot after the Next Step special is over and done with
Forum Conversation:

User1: Hey, have you guys seen the CBBC schedule for April 20th? Looks like they’re extending their broadcast hours from 7 am to 9 pm!

User2: Yeah, I saw that! It’s pretty cool that they’re adding more shows to the lineup. Did you notice that one show that usually airs in the mornings is taking the 8:30 pm slot after The Next Step special?

User3: Really? Which show is it?

User1: It’s Skunk Fu! Normally it’s on in the morning, but on April 20th, it’s going to be on at 8:30 pm. That’s a pretty big change!

User2: That’s interesting. I wonder how that’s going to work out. I guess they’re trying something new with the schedule.

User3: I’ll definitely tune in to see Skunk Fu! in the evening. It’ll be a nice change from the usual lineup. Thanks for pointing that out!

Write a forum convo saying its back to only one slot for our little stinker
Forum Conversation:

User1: Hey, did you guys catch the CBBC schedule for May 22nd?

User2: Yeah, I saw it. Looks like The Deep had its premiere today. That was exciting!

User3: I missed it! Was it good?

User1: Yeah, it was pretty good. But it looks like it’s back to only one slot for our little stinker, Skunk Fu! That means we’ll only be seeing it in the mornings from now on.

User2: Aw man, I was enjoying having it on in the evening too. Oh well, at least we still get to see it in the mornings.

User3: That’s a shame. I was getting used to having it on in the evenings. But hey, at least we can still enjoy it in the mornings. Skunk Fu! is such a fun show!
Write a follow up where 12 shows are getting new episodes through the year of 2016 and early 2017, here’s who
Follow-up Forum Conversation:

User1: Hey, did you hear the news about the upcoming episodes of some of our favorite shows on CBBC?

User2: No, what’s the scoop?

User3: Yeah, spill the beans!

User1: Well, it looks like 12 shows are getting new episodes through the year of 2016 and early 2017. Here’s the lineup: Skunk Fu!, Danger Mouse, Shaun the Sheep, Arthur, Horrible Histories, The Next Step, Operation Ouch!, Zig and Zag, Blue Peter, Hetty Feather, Wolfblood, and The Deep.

User2: That’s awesome! I love all those shows. Can’t wait to see what new adventures they have in store for us.

User3: Definitely looking forward to that. It’s going to be an exciting year for CBBC fans with all these new episodes coming our way. Time to set my DVR!
Write a convo suprised at SF getting renewed considering it’d been cancelled years ago, only started rerunning a few months ago and recently became a singular timeslot again. and there is ALREADY a “job listing” on Cartoon Saloon’s site, Also we are told when we can expect them,
Forum Conversation:

User1: Wait, did you guys see that Skunk Fu! got renewed for new episodes?

User2: What? How is that possible? I thought it had been canceled years ago.

User3: Yeah, I remember it started rerunning on CBBC just a few months ago after being off the air for so long. And now it’s already getting new episodes? That’s surprising.

User1: I know right! And get this, I saw a job listing on Cartoon Saloon’s site for animators to work on the new episodes of Skunk Fu! Looks like they’re really serious about bringing it back.

User2: That’s crazy. I wonder when we can expect to see the new episodes?

User3: According to the job listing, they’re aiming for a release in late 2016 or early 2017. So looks like we won’t have to wait too long to see more of Skunk Fu!'s adventures. Write a convo over the summer where Next Step is promoted (as usual), the new Arthur eps come straight from PBS, but some shows are slowly starting to air more

Forum Conversation:

User1: I've noticed the summer promo for CBBC is heavy on The Next Step, as usual. It seems like they're really pushing it this year.

User2: True, but have you seen that the new episodes of Arthur are coming straight from PBS? It's great that we're getting them so soon.

User3: Yeah, that's a pretty good move. But I've also noticed that some shows are starting to air more frequently. Like Horrible Histories and Danger Mouse. Seems like CBBC is shifting things a bit.

User1: Definitely, and with new episodes for a bunch of shows like Skunk Fu!, Wolfblood, and The Deep, it looks like they’re trying to refresh the daily lineup. Plus, The Deep getting that prime slot was a surprise.

User2: Totally. Although I’m all for The Next Step, it’s refreshing to see other shows getting a bit more love. Especially excited for those new Arthur episodes. It’s been a while!

User3: And let’s not forget Operation Ouch!. It's been getting more airtime too. Love that CBBC is mixing educational content with entertainment.

User1: With all these changes, this summer’s CBBC schedule is shaping up to be quite interesting. Can't wait to see how everything pans out.
Write a follow up where TNS actually LOSES one of its slots

Forum Conversation:

User1: Guys, did you catch the latest update on the CBBC schedule? It looks like The Next Step (TNS) is actually losing one of its slots!

User2: Wait, really? TNS is like one of their most promoted shows. Any idea why they decided to cut back on its airtime?

User3: I was surprised too. With all the promotion, I thought if anything, they'd add more slots, not take them away. What's going to fill its spot?

User1: From what I gathered, they're going to replace the TNS slot with a new series called "Adventure Seekers" (hypothetical show for context). It seems like they're trying to diversify the lineup more, which could be a good thing.

User2: Wow, that's a bold move. I'm all for new content, but I hope TNS fans won't be too disappointed. "Adventure Seekers" sounds interesting, though.

User3: Honestly, it might be good for CBBC to shake things up. And it's not like TNS is gone; it's just one less slot. Might make the airings a bit more special?

User1: Definitely, and who knows? “Adventure Seekers” could become the next big hit. Plus, it might give TNS fans a chance to discover something new and exciting. Change isn't always bad.

User2: Agreed. I'm curious to see how this change will affect the overall viewership and if "Adventure Seekers" will manage to captivate the TNS audience. Interesting times ahead for CBBC.
Write a staff meeting in January 2016 where TNS is renewed, but one show is renewed here as well, so this decision was made BEFORE even the Febuary change

**Staff Meeting – CBBC Headquarters, January 2016**

**Attendees**: Head of Programming, Senior Producers, Marketing Lead, Scheduling Manager

**Head of Programming**: Good morning, everyone. Let's get started. The main item on our agenda today is the renewal of our current lineup. Based on the viewership analytics and audience feedback, I'm pleased to announce that we've decided to renew "The Next Step" for another season.

**Senior Producer**: That's fantastic news. The team behind "The Next Step" will be thrilled. The show has consistently delivered strong numbers for us and has a loyal fanbase.

**Marketing Lead**: Agreed, the show's social media engagement is off the charts. It's great content for our promotional efforts. We can start planning a robust marketing campaign around the premiere.

**Head of Programming**: Definitely, but there's more. We've also decided to renew another show that's been doing well for us— "Arthur." It's been a staple in children's programming, and the new episodes from PBS have been received well.

**Scheduling Manager**: That's a solid choice. "Arthur" has a broad appeal and a cross-generational audience. It slots in nicely with our educational and entertaining content mix.

**Senior Producer**: It makes sense to continue with "Arthur." The feedback from schools and parents has been overwhelmingly positive. It's good to have more of that kind of content on the schedule.

**Marketing Lead**: With "Arthur" continuing alongside "The Next Step," it gives us a nice balance in our programming. We can cater to both the entertainment-focused audience as well as those looking for something more educational.

**Head of Programming**: Exactly. Now, in terms of scheduling and promoting these renewals, we need to consider the best approach. "The Next Step" has a prime slot, but with "Arthur," we might want to think about a morning or after-school slot, especially since the new episodes are coming straight from PBS.

**Scheduling Manager**: Agreed. I'll start looking into the scheduling specifics and see how we can optimize our lineup. For "The Next Step," we’ll keep its current slot but maybe consider a special marathon leading up to the premiere of the new season.

**Marketing Lead**: I like that idea. We can use the marathon as a launchpad for our marketing campaign. Maybe even involve some of the cast in promotional activities, both online and possibly in-person events, assuming it fits their schedule.

**Senior Producer**: Great, let's also make sure we communicate with the production teams of both shows. They'll need to know about the renewals as soon as possible to stay on track with production timelines.

**Head of Programming**: Absolutely, I'll personally reach out to both teams today and share the good news. Alright, if there are no more questions or comments, that wraps up today's meeting. Thank you all for your contributions. Let's make it another successful year.

**Meeting Adjourned.**
Write a follow up with 3 other shows that are also getting marathons

**Staff Follow-Up Meeting – CBBC Headquarters, Late January 2016**

**Attendees**: Head of Programming, Senior Producers, Marketing Lead, Scheduling Manager, Content Strategy Team

**Head of Programming**: Welcome back, team. Following our recent discussion on renewing "The Next Step" and "Arthur" and planning marathons for their new seasons, we've seen a positive response internally and from our initial outreach. Today, we need to discuss expanding that marathon strategy to include three other shows in our lineup that are performing well. 

**Scheduling Manager**: Based on our current scheduling and upcoming availabilities, I propose we consider "Horrible Histories," "Wolfblood," and "Operation Ouch!" for the next set of marathons. All three shows have dedicated fan bases and offer a mix of educational content, drama, and entertainment that could benefit from increased exposure.

**Senior Producer**: "Horrible Histories" has consistently been a hit with both kids and adults. It’s educational but fun, which fits perfectly with what we know works. A marathon could engage viewers with historical facts in an entertaining run-up to the new season.

**Head of Programming**: Agreed. "Wolfblood" has a strong following too, especially with the older kids. It’s dramatic and engaging. A marathon leading into the new season could really build anticipation.

**Marketing Lead**: And let’s not overlook "Operation Ouch!". Its appeal to curious minds about the human body and medical science is unmatched. It’s an excellent opportunity for educational engagement, particularly with interactive online content we can pair with the marathon.

**Content Strategy Team**: Each show offers unique opportunities for engagement. We could incorporate interactive quizzes for "Horrible Histories," behind-the-scenes content for "Wolfblood," and live Q&As with the doctors from "Operation Ouch!" to boost online engagement during their respective marathons.

**Scheduling Manager**: Timing will be key. We’ll need to space these marathons out to maximize viewership without overwhelming our audience. Perhaps we can schedule them around school holidays or tie them into relevant national or historical events for added traction.

**Senior Producer**: Good point. For "Horrible Histories," we could time the marathon with an upcoming historical anniversary. For "Wolfblood," aligning with a full moon could be a unique hook. As for "Operation Ouch!", perhaps during a health awareness week?

**Head of Programming**: Excellent ideas, all around. Let’s proceed with those concepts and start detailed planning. We'll need a tight collaboration between our scheduling, production, and marketing teams to pull this off successfully.

**Marketing Lead**: We’ll start drafting a comprehensive marketing strategy for each marathon, focusing on social media engagement, potential partnerships, and educational outreach. We want to create a buzz that draws in viewers and keeps them excited for what’s to come.

**Head of Programming**: Fantastic. Let’s reconvene next week with initial plans and timelines. Our aim is to provide our audience with engaging content that educates, entertains, and excites. These marathons are a key part of that strategy. If there are no further comments, let's get to work and make these marathons a success.

**Meeting Adjourned.**
Write a forum convo where some shows are suddenly gone from the lineup, one is gone for  4 dayss then coming baxk

Forum Conversation:

**User1**: Did anyone else notice that some shows suddenly disappeared from the CBBC lineup this week?

**User2**: Yeah, I was looking for "Wolfblood" and couldn't find it anywhere. It's like it vanished into thin air!

**User3**: "Horrible Histories" is gone too. My kids and I had a mini marathon planned for the weekend. 😔

**User4**: It's weird because "Operation Ouch!" is also missing. There was no announcement or anything. Anyone know what's going on?

**User5**: I read somewhere that "Wolfblood" will be back in 4 days. Seems like it's just a temporary thing. Maybe they're rearranging the schedule?

**User1**: Only 4 days for "Wolfblood"? That's a relief but still, some heads up would've been nice. Hope the others come back soon too.

**User2**: Is it possible they're making room for something new or perhaps special programming? I noticed they sometimes do that, but there's usually an announcement.

**User3**: True, CBBC usually lets us know. I checked their social media and website; not a word about these changes. It's all very sudden.

**User4**: Maybe it's some kind of technical issue or licensing hiccup? Those can cause unexpected schedule changes.

**User5**: Good point, @User4. Could be a technical problem. Wouldn't be the first time a channel had to temporarily pull shows for reasons like that.

**User1**: Whatever the reason, I just hope CBBC communicates what's happening soon. It's confusing for the viewers, especially the younger audience.

**User2**: Agreed. Communication is key. At least we know "Wolfblood" is coming back. Fingers crossed for "Horrible Histories" and "Operation Ouch!" too.

**User3**: For now, it looks like we'll have to wait and see. Hopefully, there will be an update or some clarity from CBBC in the coming days.

**User4**: Yeah, in the meantime, it might be a good chance to explore some other shows. Any recommendations?

**User5**: If you haven't checked out "The Next Step" yet, now's a good time. Also, "Arthur" has some timeless episodes that are always worth a watch.

**User1**: Thanks for the suggestions! I'll definitely check those out. Let's keep each other posted if we hear anything new about the missing shows.

**User2**: Will do. Here's hoping for some good news soon. 🤞
Write a follow up with the 4 network darlings as OO seems to be losing steam,

Forum Conversation Follow-Up:

**User1**: Hey everyone, have you noticed the recent lineup changes on CBBC seem to be solidifying? Looks like the network has their four darlings, but "Operation Ouch!" seems to be losing some steam.

**User2**: Yeah, I saw that. "The Next Step", "Horrible Histories", "Wolfblood", and "Arthur" are practically the channel's flagship shows now. They're being promoted heavily.

**User3**: It's kind of sad about "Operation Ouch!". It used to be everywhere on the channel. The educational content was spot on, and my kids learned a lot. Wonder why it's fading into the background.

**User4**: Maybe it's just the natural ebb and flow of TV. Some shows capture the zeitgeist for a while then make way for others. "Operation Ouch!" had a good run, but the other four have broader appeal maybe?

**User5**: I think it's also about viewer engagement. "The Next Step" and "Horrible Histories" have huge followings, and "Wolfblood" has that cult status. "Arthur" is timeless. "Operation Ouch!" is brilliant, but it might not drive the same level of constant engagement.

**User1**: True. Plus, the shift might be down to changes in what kids are interested in watching. The network darlings offer a mix of drama, history, and relatable situations. Maybe "Operation Ouch!" doesn't fit the current trend as well?

**User2**: It's a pity though. Educational shows like "Operation Ouch!" are necessary. They add so much value beyond entertainment. Hopefully, CBBC finds a way to keep blending those in.

**User3**: Agreed. There's always a cycle, though. Who knows? Maybe there'll be a resurgence of interest in educational content and "Operation Ouch!" will make a comeback.

**User4**: Networks are all about ratings and engagement, sadly. But I do believe there's always a space for educational programming. We might see "Operation Ouch!" or something similar take off again in the future.

**User5**: In the meantime, let's continue to support our favorite shows and maybe get more creative with how we engage with educational content at home. The digital space offers a lot of options.

**User1**: Good point, @User5. There's a vast amount of resources online. And who knows, maybe this shift at CBBC might inspire new educational content that we haven't seen before.

**User2**: Let's keep our fingers crossed for the return of educational programming to the forefront. Kids need fun learning just as much as entertainment.

**User3**: Absolutely. Meanwhile, we'll stay tuned to see how the channel evolves and keep supporting our favorites.
Write a follow up with the new schedule from Feb 3 2017

Forum Conversation Follow-Up: New Schedule from February 3, 2017

**User1**: Hey everyone, have you seen the new CBBC schedule that rolled out today, February 3, 2017? Looks like there are some significant changes.

**User2**: Yeah, I just checked it out. They really mixed things up this time. "The Next Step" and "Horrible Histories" are still holding strong in their prime slots. 

**User3**: Noticed that too. But what really caught my eye was the addition of some new shows to the lineup. Seems like CBBC is trying to freshen things up. 

**User4**: "Wolfblood" and "Arthur" are still there, but I saw they've adjusted their airing times slightly. Hopefully, it doesn't affect their viewership negatively. 

**User5**: Did anyone else see the new educational show they've added? Looks like they might be trying to fill the void left by "Operation Ouch!" losing steam. I'm here for it; always good to have more educational content.

**User1**: Absolutely, @User5. It's crucial they maintain a balance between entertainment and education. The new show seems promising based on the description.

**User2**: I’m just glad they're still committed to including a variety of genres in the mix. Keeps things interesting for viewers of all ages.

**User3**: It’ll be interesting to see how the audience reacts to the new schedule. Hoping the changes bring in more viewers and don't alienate the loyal fans.

**User4**: One thing I've always appreciated about CBBC is their ability to adapt and try new things. They're not afraid to shake up the schedule if it might benefit the audience.

**User5**: True. And let's not forget the special events they occasionally run. Those always bring a bit of excitement to the regular schedule. I wonder if there are any planned soon.

**User1**: Good point. They’ve been pretty quiet about special events for now, but I'm sure they've got a few surprises up their sleeve for 2017. 

**User2**: In the meantime, let's keep supporting our favorite shows. Who knows, our feedback might influence future scheduling decisions.

**User3**: Definitely. And let's give the new shows a chance too. I'm looking forward to seeing what new content CBBC has in store for us.

**User4**: Agreed! Here’s to hoping 2017 brings even more great programming to CBBC. Cheers to new beginnings and fresh content!

**User5**: Cheers to that! Can't wait to check out the new lineup in depth. Here’s to another year of awesome shows on CBBC!
Write the new schedule

Given the evolving nature of television programming and the need for this response to create a fictional, up-to-date schedule for CBBC on February 3, 2017, I'll outline a versatile and engaging lineup tailored for the channel's diverse audience. This schedule includes a blend of returning favorites, educational content, and fresh new shows to keep the lineup exciting and relevant.

### CBBC Schedule for February 3, 2017

**Morning**

- 07:00 am - **Blue Peter** (A classic show kicking off the day with interesting crafts, projects, and guest appearances)
- 07:30 am - **Class Dismissed** (Comedy sketches set in a fictional school, perfect for kids starting their day)
- 08:00 am - **Danger Mouse** (Adventures of the greatest secret agent, blending humor and action)
- 08:30 am - **Shaun the Sheep** (Aardman's clever sheep gets into more silent but hilarious antics)

**Mid-Morning**

- 09:00 am - **Horrible Histories** (Educational fun exploring the silliest aspects of history)
- 09:30 am - **Art Ninja** (Creative projects intertwined with comedy and animation)
- 10:00 am - **Operation Ouch!** (Insightful look into the human body with fun experiments and facts)
- 10:30 am - **My Life** (Documentary series showcasing unique stories from children's perspectives)

**Lunchtime**

- 11:00 am - **The Next Step** (The drama of a dance studio, focusing on friendship, competition, and perseverance)
- 11:30 am - **Wolfblood** (A series blending teen drama with the supernatural world of werewolves)

**Afternoon**

- 12:00 pm - **So Awkward** (A comedy series following the lives of three socially awkward friends)
- 12:30 pm - **Hetty Feather** (Adventures and challenges of a spirited girl in Victorian London)
- 01:00 pm - **Mystic** (Drama about a girl and her bond with horses, blending environmental themes)
- 01:30 pm - **Young Dracula** (Comedy-drama focusing on the young Vlad Dracula trying to live a normal life)

**Early Evening**

- 02:00 pm - **Arthur** (The adventures of Arthur Read, an aardvark, navigating childhood challenges)
- 02:30 pm - **Danger Mouse** (Repeat)
- 03:00 pm - **Dragons: Race to the Edge** (Animated series following Hiccup and Toothless)
- 03:30 pm - **Jamie Johnson** (A drama about a young footballer's dreams and struggles)

**Prime Time**

- 04:00 pm - **Newsround** (News and current affairs presented in an accessible manner for children)
- 04:15 pm - **The Dumping Ground** (Stories from the residents of a care home for children)
- 05:00 pm - **Now You See It** (Magic show highlighting tricks and optical illusions)
- 05:30 pm - **The Deep** (Animated adventures of the Nekton family exploring the ocean's depths)

---

Please note, this fictionalized schedule aims to showcase a blend of genres from action-packed adventures and educational content to heartfelt dramas, ensuring that there's something for every young viewer throughout the day.
Write a tweet with a show that had its new eps SNUBBED

"Can't believe #CBBC just snubbed new episodes of #OperationOuch 😱! We were all set for some mind-blowing medical mysteries and fun facts that make learning a blast. Bring back our fave docs! 👩‍⚕️👨‍⚕️ #BringBackOperationOuch #KidsTV"
Write a tweet about what happened with Skunk

"Just heard about #SkunkFu's surprise comeback then sudden hiatus on #CBBC 😔. From reruns to revival talks and then silence - what's up? Our mornings miss that adventurous skunk! 🦨💥 Fans deserve answers and more episodes! 🌟 #SaveSkunkFu #KidsTV"
